title: Lucid Motors Cuts 12% of Workforce in Profitability Push
text: Lucid Motors announced a 12% workforce reduction as part of a broader profitability push following disappointing Q1 2026 results. The reduction was aimed at generating up to $500 million in cost savings over three years. The company had reported burning through nearly $1 billion in losses during Q1 2026, with cash burn rates making the workforce reduction necessary to extend operational runway. New CEO Silvio Napoli's strategic review identified the workforce reduction as critical to achieving cost discipline and moving toward profitability. The cuts represented one of the most significant restructuring efforts since the company's public listing and reflected the severity of the financial challenges faced by the EV manufacturer.
